# willActivate(withUser:)

**Instance Method**

Tells the authorization plug-in that its user interface is about to be made active by the Apple-provided Security Agent.

## Declaration

```swift
func willActivate(withUser inUserInformation: [AnyHashable : Any]!)
```

## Parameters

- **inUserInformation**: A dictionary that contains the following information:

- [doc://com.apple.securityinterface/documentation/SecurityInterface/SFAuthorizationPluginViewUserNameKey]

An [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Foundation/NSString] object containing the selected user’s name

- [doc://com.apple.securityinterface/documentation/SecurityInterface/SFAuthorizationPluginViewUserShortNameKey]

An [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Foundation/NSString] object containing the selected user’s short name

Note: `inUserInformation` may be `nil`.

## Discussion

Your [doc://com.apple.securityinterface/documentation/SecurityInterface/SFAuthorizationPluginView] instance can use the user name to pre-populate a text field in the user interface.

## Configuring the User Interface

- **didActivate()**: Tells the authorization plug-in when its user interface has become active.
- **didDeactivate()**: Tells the authorization plug-in that its user interface has been deactivated.
